instruction: Implement to_roman(n) converting an integer 1..3999 to its Roman numeral string, including subtractive forms: to_roman(4)=='IV', to_roman(9)=='IX', to_roman(40)=='XL', to_roman(58)=='LVIII', to_roman(1994)=='MCMXCIV'.

files.test_roman.py:
from roman import to_roman


def test_roman():
    assert to_roman(4) == "IV"
    assert to_roman(9) == "IX"
    assert to_roman(58) == "LVIII"
    assert to_roman(1994) == "MCMXCIV"
